mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term equity price appreciation over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO momentum falls below 0.02 during tightening periods, or 5-day returns are uncorrelated with production growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to adopt restrictive policy stances, increasing borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets post positive 20-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation months without subsequent rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate profit margins and cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDP growth across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 0.5 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates supply-side stagnation and weakening economic momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO quarter-over-quarter growth falls below 0.5 percent
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when industrial production growth crosses below the threshold.
